blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Things. Your mom. She just looks at you and she already knows.
He thought about the raised eyebrow, the small smile. Maybe Daisy was right. He didn't always know what it was his mom knew, but she usually knew something.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She's always said you're the sensible one.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: You're not supposed to agree with that.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Why not? It's true. She's been watching you make terrible decisions for twelve years and she's looking at me like I'm the reason they don't get worse.
She walked for a second. The lamplight they were passing through made her shadow stretch long and then shorter and then long again.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You don't have to say that.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm not saying it to be nice. I'm saying it because it's true.

She didn't answer. They turned onto her block and the night insects got louder, the way they always did on this particular street, and he didn't know if that was just how he remembered it or if it was actually true.
